summary:
The primary goal for treating this extensively involved laryngeal cancer should be precise removal of tumors while maintaining function; thus, \textbf{vertical hemilaryngectomy} stands out as the best option among available treatments considering both effectiveness against cancer spread and retention of speech capabilities post-surgery. 

The chosen answer aligns closely with Agent2’s recommendation by emphasizing precision surgery tailored towards minimizing side effects yet ensuring adequate clearance of malignancy within an anatomically challenging area such as the larynx where critical structures are at stake.
